title: Roldana floresiorum
taxonomy: Roldana floresiorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Mexico and is a member of the Roldana genus, which includes various species of shrubby plants with daisy-like flowers. The classification of Roldana floresiorum is as a species within the subgenus Roldana and the section Roldana. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the order Asterales. The classification of Roldana floresiorum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Roldana floresiorum, also known as the Mexican gold flower, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, infections, and various gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Roldana floresiorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues.
